degraded: E2E spec 73 (pre-existing) — its 2 pty-dependent cases fail on the isolated stack with pty_spawn_failed code 5 (ACCESS_DENIED); 9 of 11 pass. PROVEN ENVIRONMENTAL, not caused by this change: the same 2 cases fail identically on unmodified code, while @lydell/node-pty spawns pwsh.exe fine standalone on the same machine and temp profile. Spec 77 was therefore designed to need no live pty.

behaviors.behavior: Reconnect scheduler settles into a capped tail instead of terminating
behaviors.evidence: useTerminalSocket.osresume.test.ts > AC-1 never inert — getTimerCount()>0 + exactly +1 attempt per tail interval — PASS

behaviors.behavior: Network returning after the ramp recovers the terminal unaided
behaviors.evidence: osresume > REPRO network-returns — PASS (FAILED before the fix)

behaviors.behavior: `online` re-arms and reconnects immediately
behaviors.evidence: osresume > REPRO online — PASS (FAILED before the fix)

behaviors.behavior: `online` recovery cancels the armed tail — no delayed second attach
`online` leaves a healthy OPEN socket alone — no double attach
Tail never resurrects a replay-only attach on an ABNORMAL close
`online` never resurrects a replay-only attach
Refocus still reconnects immediately, not via the tail
`reconnecting` true while a retry is armed, false on recovery
A replay-only attach never claims to be reconnecting
Watchdog reaps an attempt stuck in CONNECTING so retry resumes
behaviors.evidence: osresume > AC-1 hung connect — PASS (fails without the watchdog)

behaviors.behavior: Delay policy: ramp -> tail -> slow tail; never non-positive; monotonic
isSlowTail boundary agrees with the delay policy
Watchdog: spares OPEN/superseded/cancelled, idempotent clear, re-arm replaces, swallows close() throw
Reconnecting banner renders with the no-reload advice
Banner absent when the socket is healthy
Banner renders ABOVE read-only (no connection outranks role)
Banner softens copy on the slow tail, dropping the 'not needed' promise
Banner grace: no arm before window; arms after; disarms on recovery; blip clears the timer
REAL BROWSER: client keeps retrying past the old 5-attempt budget
behaviors.evidence: e2e/flows/77-terminal-reconnect-after-outage.spec.ts — PASS; verified load-bearing by restoring the old cap and rebuilding: FAILED with 'Expected: > 6, Received: 6'

behaviors.behavior: REAL BROWSER: outage surfaces as the reconnecting banner, calm cadence (<1 attempt/s)
Real macOS lid-close -> Tailscale re-establish -> unaided recovery on the reporter's hardware
behaviors.evidence: Needs a genuinely suspending Mac plus a real tailnet renegotiation; neither CI nor a local harness can produce an OS suspend and a tunnel returning. The client-side property it depends on (a retry is always armed, and any later success reconnects) is pinned by the rows above.

surface_verification.justification: WS-layer change, so the backend-affects-frontend rule applies and jsdom cannot prove the outcome. Executed e2e/flows/77-terminal-reconnect-after-outage.spec.ts in real Chromium against an isolated single-process stack (temp USERPROFILE, built client via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono 127.0.0.1:3849, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local): 1 passed. Verified LOAD-BEARING by restoring the old 5-attempt cap in useTerminalSocket.ts, rebuilding the client and re-running: FAILED with 'Expected: > 6, Received: 6' — the attempt count freezing at 6 is exactly the reported defect. Fix restored and re-run green.
